seoTitle: Mount Field vs Mount Hicks — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Field and Mount Hicks. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Field vs Mount Hicks: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Field and Mount Hicks.
heading: Mount Field vs Mount Hicks — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Field, Tasmania and Mount Hicks, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Field has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Mount Hicks (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Field or Mount Hicks safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Field has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Mount Hicks scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Field scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Hicks scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Field or Mount Hicks?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Field has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
